决策对我来说一直很困难，尤其是当我不知所措时。例如，我在餐厅门口分手，因为我无法决定要进去还是选择其他餐厅。
 在远程工作和社交隔离之间，我今年花了很多时间。最难的部分是需要为自己做出更多的决定。我必须决定何时起床，吃什么，做什么工作以及如何利用业余时间。当然，以前这些基本上都由我控制，但我在很大程度上依赖于社会压力来选择。
 在研究Adam进行访谈时，我找到了他的用户指南，并决定为自己编写一个。他和第一轮审阅稿的原始作者都是从经理的角度编写的，但是用户指南对个人贡献者同样有用。重点是分享与您的最佳合作方式，这在面对面人数较少的团队时尤其重要。
 我创建了一个模板（可以在此处复制），然后卡住了。每个要点都代表我需要对自己做出的决定。我的起步工作要求我不断做出决定，而我实际上很喜欢这个过程。但是当我个人决定时，我会感到完全不同。
 用户指南本质上是一种品牌练习，我担心自己的选择会被骗子。我称自己为早起的人，积极进取的人和工作狂。但是我是在晚上的最后一刻写这本书的，整个周末都没有工作（尽管有时也不能放松）。我的故事是基于真理还是抱负？
 决策也使我们容易遭到拒绝。通过列出我的喜好，性格特征和宠儿，我发布了一种快速的方式来判断我。如果有人看到了这个并且不想和我一起工作怎么办？
 通常，决策对我来说很自私。当我决定在一天中的什么时间喜欢开会时，我对自己的需求优先于别人的需求感到内。编写用户指南会迫使您面对自己讲述的故事。而且您必须足够脆弱，以至于人们实际上可以通过阅读所写内容中学到一些有用的东西。 
一年前，我与一个从未做出任何决定的人有过关系。 现在我看到不做决定是自私的。 而且我不想那样。  最后，这是我的用户指南，因为它比清单更能说明我。
note_en:
Decisions have always been hard for me, especially when I'm overwhelmed. For example, I break down in front of restaurants because I can't decide if I want to go in or pick a different restaurant.
 Between working remotely and social distancing, I have spent a lot of time alone this year. The hardest part is needing to make way more decisions for myself. I have to decide when to get up, what to eat, what to work on, and what to do with my free time. Sure, these were largely in my control before, but I relied a lot on social pressure to choose.
 When I was researching   Adam  for   our interview , I found his user guide   and decided to write one for myself. Both his and the one from the original   First Round Review piece  are written from the perspective of a manager, but user guides can be just as helpful for individual contributors. The point is sharing how best to work with you, which is especially important at a time when fewer teams are in-person.
 I created a template (  which you can copy here ) and then got stuck. Every bullet point represents a decision that I need to make about myself. My startup job requires me to make decisions constantly, and I actually enjoy that process. But I feel entirely different when it’s a personal decision.
 User guides are essentially a branding exercise, and I worry that I will get called a liar for my choices. I brand myself as a morning person, a proactive doer, and a workaholic. But I’m writing this at night, at the last minute, having spent all weekend   not  working (though also somehow not relaxing). Are my stories based on truth or aspiration?
 Decisions also open us up to rejection. By listing my preferences, personality traits, and pet peeves, I’m publishing a quick way to judge me. What if someone sees this and doesn’t want to work with me?
 Often decisions feel selfish to me. When I decide what time of day I prefer meetings, I feel guilty for prioritizing my own wants over someone else’s. Writing a user guide forces you to confront the stories you tell about yourself. And you have to be vulnerable enough that people will actually learn something useful from reading what you write.
 A year ago I was in a relationship with someone who never decided anything. Now I see that   not  deciding was selfish. And I don’t want to be that way.
 In the end, this piece is my user guide, because it says more about me than a checklist can.
title: 您应该编写用户指南
title_en: You Should Write a User Guide

互联网权利运动人士称，Twitter处理版权侵权的政策正越来越多地用于恶意攻击坦桑尼亚人权活动家开立的帐户，以使其沉默。
 每天在Twitter上，Kigogo-斯瓦希里语的名字，意思是VIP或贪婪的大亨-散布坦桑尼亚权力走廊中的最新八卦。
 这些细节有时令人尴尬和震惊，但Kigogo的近40万Twitter追随者喜欢这些启示，称其为我们的Twitter共和国总裁Kigogo。
   我是举报人，我揭露了该国的腐败和侵犯人权行为，＆＃34; Kigogo的身份是一个严密的秘密，他告诉BBC。
 但是在10月28日选举前不久，Twitter暂停了@ Kigogo2014帐户，原因是该帐户有300多个＆＃34;向社交媒体平台投诉该帐户违反了其版权政策，Kigogo否认了这一指控。
 互联网权利运动人士声称，该政策越来越被“专制政府”使用。例如坦桑尼亚使评论家保持沉默。
 Twitter并未直接回应这些指控，而是在十月份发布了声明，谴责选举前封锁社交媒体平台的行为。 
在Kigogo描述的一个实例中，来自@ Kigogo2014的1000多条推文被复制并用于建立三个网站，投诉人随后使用这些网站说其版权已被侵犯。
 ＆＃34;帐户被暂停后，我联系了Twitter，但这就像我在处理机器人而不是人类。我正在收到程序化的响应。没有人的大脑参与其中，＆＃34;子吾说。
 袭击发生在几天后，Kigogo在推特上发表了关于执政党据称计划在选举前篡改选票的计划，几天前，约翰·马格富里总统正在寻求连任。
   申诉人呼吁Twitter严厉打击Kigogo，因为它违反了美国的《数字千年版权法案》（DMCA），Twitter和其他美国知名的科技公司必须遵守该法案。
  但是，互联网维权组织Access Now表示，人们越来越多地使用虚假的DMCA声明，以试图压制报道侵犯人权行为的记者，活动家和民间社会组织。
  该组织说，它已经处理了9起案件，其中版权投诉被用于针对坦桑尼亚的帐户。
 ＆＃34;在Twitter暂停我的帐户后，我被要求提供我的个人信息，例如我的地址和电话号码，他们说这些信息将与投诉人共享，＆＃34;子吾说。 
＆＃34;坦桑尼亚情报人员的策略是找出有关我的更多细节，以帮助他们揭露我的身份，但我拒绝遵守。
   Kigogo说，在十月份的攻击之前，该帐户已被坦桑尼亚情报部门三次入侵。
 英国广播公司（BBC）要求推特发表评论，但该公司的一位发言人向我们介绍了该公司在坦桑尼亚大选前夕发布的一条推文，敦促政府解除互联网关闭。
    电话用户被阻止发送批量文本消息，其中一些声称包含反对派领导人姓名的SMS被阻止。
 Facebook，Twitter和Instagram等主要社交媒体平台被阻止或限制。尽管有些人正在使用虚拟专用网络（VPN）来克服封锁，但仍然很难在手机上访问Twitter。
 荷兰驻坦桑尼亚大使最近问，为什么仍然限制社交媒体平台。
   根据互联网权利组织Access Now法律部门负责人彼得·米塞克（Peter Micek）的说法，坦桑尼亚的电信监管机构据说安装了可能扼杀整个网络，阻塞网站并降低流量的设备，从而无法传输视频或照片。 
敌人和支持者都参与了有关@ Kigogo2014 Twitter帐户背后的人及其所在地的疯狂猜测。
 有人说，这是由关系密切的州官员或代理人经营的。
 其他人试图通过使用嘻哈歌手Jay Z的漫画化面孔作为该帐户的个人资料照片寻找线索，该照片已被多个Kigogo模仿者帐户使用。
   坦桑尼亚的几名官员曾公开表示，他们正在寻找开通Twitter帐户的人，指控他们煽动，泄露政府机密，散布谎言并威胁国家安全。
 自从Magufuli总统于2015年上任以来，对主流媒体和政府批评家的镇压日益加剧，经常将Kigogo的推文复制并转发到WhatsApp，将其提供给并行信息系统。
 玛格夫利先生的政府在10月份有争议的选举中赢得第二任期之前，通过了诸如《在线内容法规》和《网络犯罪法》之类的法律，该法律要求开发所有博客作者的在线注册簿，并安装所有网吧闭路电视摄像机，对不遵守规定的摄像机处以罚款。
     媒体业务被政府关闭的记者玛丽亚·萨隆吉·谢海（Maria Sarungi Tsehai）告诉英国广播公司，推特是许多有影响力的坦桑尼亚人认为可以聚集在一起表达意见的唯一地方。 
因此，在选举前几天遭到版权投诉后，Kigogo和其他五个著名的Twitter帐户被暂停后，她无法保持沉默。
 Tsehai女士写信给Twitter的创始人兼首席执行官Jack Dorsey公开信后，要求他停止压制和通过您善意的版权侵权管理系统对我们进行沉默，并恢复了帐户并借助Access Now进行了验证。
  尽管Kigogo对该帐户的附加保护感到放心。 Kigogo表示，Twitter现在已经意识到其版权政策的恶意滥用，该公司应该做更多的事情。
 ＆＃34;情况只有在玛丽亚（Maria）写信给杰克·多尔西（Jack Dorsey）之后才改变，但我认为他们需要加强检查版权声明的尽职调查系统。
 例如，如果是一家公司，则需要审核和确认所有使该公司合法化的文件，这是他们制止虚假主张的唯一方法。
 Access Now认为，坦桑尼亚对版权法的使用可能会在其他非洲国家复制。
 ＆＃34;当局利用坦桑尼亚的版权漏洞的趋势越来越令人担忧，并且对整个大陆的言论自由构成威胁，＆＃34; Access Now的Berhan Taye告诉BBC。 
她补充说：“受版权漏洞影响的社交媒体平台别无选择，只能紧急解决此问题，并帮助确保政府不再对批评者采取这种策略。”  Kigogo继续用多汁的细节向他的追随者开枪，但也对Twitter帐户所创造的奥秘感到满意，并希望它继续让坦桑尼亚人猜测哪个代词最能描述他/她/他们是谁。  英国广播公司已要求坦桑尼亚政府发言人对本文中的指控发表评论，但到目前为止，我们尚未收到任何答复。
note_en:
Twitter's policy that deals with the infringement of copyright is increasingly being used to maliciously target accounts run by Tanzanian human rights activists in order to silence them, internet rights campaigners allege.
 Every day on Twitter, Kigogo - a Swahili name that means a VIP or swashbuckling tycoon - doles out the latest gossip from Tanzania's corridors of power.
 The details are embarrassing and shocking at times but Kigogo's nearly 400,000 Twitter followers love these revelations, dubbing Kigogo "our president of the Twitter republic".
   "I'm a whistleblower and I expose corruption and human rights abuses in the country," Kigogo, whose identity is a closely guarded secret, told the BBC.
 But shortly before the 28 October election, Twitter suspended the @Kigogo2014 account because of "more than 300" complaints to the social media platform that the account had breached its copyright policy - a charge Kigogo denied.
 Internet rights campaigners allege that the policy is increasingly being used by "repressive governments" such as Tanzania's to silence critics.
 Twitter has not responded directly to these allegations but did release a statement in October decrying the blocking of the social media platform ahead of the election.
 In one instance that Kigogo described, more than 1,000 tweets from @Kigogo2014 were copied and used to set up three websites, the complainants then used those websites to say that their copyright had been breached.
 "After my account was suspended I reached out to Twitter but it was like I was dealing with bots not humans. I was getting programmed responses. No human brain was involved," Kigogo said.
 The attack came days after Kigogo tweeted about an alleged scheme by the ruling party to tamper with ballot papers ahead of the elections, in which President John Magufuli was seeking a second term.
   The complainants appealed to Twitter to crack down on Kigogo for violating the US's Digital Millennium Copyright Act, or DMCA, which Twitter and other popular US-based technology companies have to comply with around the world.
  But internet rights advocacy group Access Now says it's seeing the increased use of  spurious DMCA claims in attempts to silence journalists, activists, and civil society organisations that report on human rights violations.
  The organisation says it has dealt with nine cases where copyright complaints have been used to target accounts from Tanzania.
 "After Twitter suspended my account I was asked to give my personal information like my address and phone number which they said will be shared with the complainant," Kigogo said.
 "The strategy here was for the Tanzanian intelligence to find out more details about me that would help them unmask my identity, but I refused to comply."
   Before the October attack "the account had been hacked... three times by, I suspect, Tanzanian intelligence", Kigogo said.
 The BBC asked Twitter for a comment but a spokesperson for the company referred us to a tweet the company posted on the eve of the Tanzania election urging the government to lift an internet shutdown.
    Phone users were blocked from sending bulk text messages with some claiming SMSs containing names of opposition leaders were blocked.
 The main social media platforms like Facebook, Twitter and Instagram were either blocked or restricted. It remains extremely difficult to access Twitter on a mobile phone, although some people are using Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) to beat the blockade.
 The Dutch ambassador to Tanzania recently asked why the social media platform was still restricted.
   According to Peter Micek, head of the legal arm of internet rights group Access Now,  Tanzania's telecoms regulator had allegedly installed equipment  that could throttle entire networks, block websites and degrade traffic so that video or photos could not be transmitted.
  Foes and supporters alike have been involved in frenzied speculation about the person behind @Kigogo2014 Twitter account and where they were based.
 Some say it is being run by a well-connected state official or by a proxy.
 Others have attempted to look for clues in the use of the caricatured face of hip-pop artist Jay Z as the account's profile photo, an image that has been used by several Kigogo copycat accounts.
   Several Tanzanian officials have publicly said they were looking for the person running the Twitter account, accusing them of incitement, leaking government secrets, spreading lies and threatening national security.
 Kigogo's tweets are often copied and forwarded on WhatsApp feeding a parallel information system in a country where there's been an intensified crackdown on mainstream media and government critics since President Magufuli came into office in 2015.
 Before he won a second term in the controversial election in October, Mr Magufuli's government passed laws such as the  Online Content Regulations and Cyber Crimes Act which require the development of an online register of all bloggers and for all cyber cafes to install CCTV cameras and impose fines on those that fail to comply.
     Journalist Maria Sarungi Tsehai, whose media business was shut down by the government, told the BBC that Twitter was the only place many influential Tanzanians felt they could gather to express their views.
  She therefore couldn't stay quiet after Kigogo and five other prominent Twitter accounts were suspended after copyright complaints days before the election.
 Shortly after Ms Tsehai  wrote an open letter to Twitter's founder and CEO Jack Dorsey, asking him "to stop repression and our silencing through your well meaning system of managing copyright violations", the accounts were reinstated and some verified with the help of Access Now.
  Although Kigogo feels reassured about the account's "added protection" and that Twitter was now aware of the nefarious misuse of its copyright policy, Kigogo says the company should do more.
 "Things only changed after Maria wrote to Jack Dorsey, but I think they need to strengthen their due diligence system of checking copyright claims.
 "If, for example, it's a company then all documents that legitimise that company need to be vetted and confirmed, that's the only way they can stop the fake claims."
 Access Now believes that Tanzania's use of copyright law might be replicated in other African countries.
 "The increasing trend of authorities exploiting copyright loopholes in Tanzania is incredibly concerning and poses a threat to freedom of speech across the entire continent," Berhan Taye from Access Now told the BBC.
 She added: "Social media platforms affected by the copyright loophole have no choice but to urgently address this issue, and help ensure governments can no longer deploy this tactic against their critics."
 Kigogo continues to regale his followers with juicy details but also relishes in the mystery the Twitter account has created and hopes it continues to keep Tanzanians guessing which pronoun best describes who he/she/they is or are.
 The BBC has asked the spokesperson of the Tanzanian government for comment on the allegations made in this article, but we have not received a response so far.
title: 互联网人权运动人士声称坦桑尼亚正在使用Twitter的版权政策，以人权活动分子开辟的恶意帐户为目标，以使其沉默
title_en: Internet rights campaigners allege Tanzania is using Twitter's copyright policy to maliciously target accounts run by human rights activists to silence them

预计交易将于2021年第二季度完成后，Vimeo将成为一家独立的公开交易公司。
 IAC表示，在从Thrive Capital和GIC筹集了1.5亿美元的股权之后，Vimeo在11月初获得了27.5亿美元的估值。
 IAC周二宣布将剥离其视频软件公司Vimeo的全部股份。
   交易完成后，预计将于2021年第二季度完成，Vimeo将成为一家独立的上市公司。
 该公司表示，预计此次交易将采用IAC股票的重新分类形式。
 计划中的分拆计划是在软件即服务（SaaS）库存飙升之际进行的，其中部分原因是Covid-19大流行。 Vimeo在过去一年中报告了强劲的用户增长，11月的用户增长了24％。 Vimeo以前专注于流媒体娱乐，特别是来自独立电影制片人的流媒体娱乐，几年前改变了其计划，为企业提供视频服务。
 IAC表示，在从Thrive Capital和GIC筹集了1.5亿美元的股权之后，该公司在11月初实现了27.5亿美元的估值。 
这将使Vimeo成为从IAC分离出来的第11家上市公司。 该公司最后同意分拆在线约会公司Match Group的所有股份。
note_en:
Following completion of the deal, which is expect to occur in the second quarter of 2021, Vimeo will become an independent, publicly traded company.
 Vimeo achieved a $2.75 billion valuation in early November IAC said, after raising $150 million in equity from Thrive Capital and GIC.
 IAC is spinning off its full stake in its video software company, Vimeo, the company announced Tuesday.
   Following completion of the deal, which is expect to close in the second quarter of 2021, Vimeo will become an independent, publicly traded company.
 The transaction is expected to take the form of a reclassification of IAC shares, the company said.
 The planned spinoff comes as software as a service (SaaS) stocks are soaring, boosted in part by the Covid-19 pandemic. Vimeo has reported strong user growth over the past year, with subscribers up 24% in November. Vimeo, which previously focused on streaming entertainment, especially from independent filmmakers, pivoted its plan a few years ago to provide video services for businesses.
 The company achieved a  $2.75 billion valuation in early November, after raising $150 million in equity from Thrive Capital and GIC, IAC said.
 This will make Vimeo the 11th public company to spin out from IAC. The company last  agreed to spin off all of its shares of online dating company  Match Group.
title: IAC表示计划剥离其在Vimeo的全部股份，使其在2021年第二季度成为一家独立的公开贸易公司。 IAC股票上涨8.5％+
title_en: IAC says it plans to spin off its full stake in Vimeo, making it an independent, publicly trade company in Q2 2021; IAC stock rises 8.5%+

note:
当这家科技巨头提供早期和平报价时，州和联邦调查人员正在对针对Facebook的两个具有里程碑意义的反托拉斯诉讼进行画龙点睛：这将有助于其下一个竞争对手站起来。
  几个月以来，华盛顿和全国各地的监管机构一直在调查Facebook及其首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）有关该公司已成为非法垄断的指控。监管者越来越相信，这家拥有近17年历史的社交网络巨头已经系统地寻求收购或淘汰其所有竞争对手，并采取非法手段成为世界上最赚钱的数字服务之一。
 Facebook长期以来一直对此说法提出异议。据三位知情人士说，为了说明其对竞争的承诺，该公司的高级律师表示，他们愿意改变其某些商业惯例。个人说，Facebook提出的想法之一将允许另一家公司或开发人员许可访问其强大的代码以及用户的错综复杂的关系网，以便他们可以更轻松地创建自己的社交网络版本。在不愿透露姓名的情况下发言，描述了一项非公共执法调查。
   但是调查人员最终拒绝了这个主意，认为Facebook的提议未能完全解决他们的竞争担忧。Facebook的提议是该技术巨头今年提出的含糊的早期补救措施的一部分。最终，联邦贸易委员会和纽约州莱蒂西亚·詹姆斯（Letitia James）领导的几乎每个州的检察长本月提出了两起诉讼，力图使Facebook与众不同。
 在州和联邦调查的最后几天，Facebook紧张的最后一刻谈判显示了该公司寻求拒绝政府指控的紧迫性-并预示了将在战斗中部署的激进策略。十几个人称，在长达18个月的调查中，Facebook加大了游说力度，聘请了有能力的前政府反托拉斯律师，并启动了一系列针对竞争问题的内部举措。诉讼中的问题。
 现在，Facebook的努力为数字时代最严重的法律冲突之一奠定了基础：一场约8000亿美元的科技巨头与美国一些最强大的监管机构之间的法庭对决。结果不仅会影响Facebook的未来，还会影响整个硅谷–以及政府监管它的能力。
   民主党参议员理查德·布卢门撒尔（Richard Blumenthal）说：“执法人员摆脱了数十年来给予这些公司通行证的自满情绪，”他曾在康涅狄格州担任总检察长20年，然后在十年前进入国会。 
这些反托拉斯诉讼的主要目的是针对Facebook收购当时两个新兴的社交服务：照片共享应用Instagram和消息传递工具WhatsApp。 Facebook分别在2012年和2014年以数十亿美元的交易收购了这两家公司，FTC当时对此进行了审查，但当时并未阻止。
 从那以后，州和联邦反托拉斯执法者争辩说，这些交易最终成为麻烦的，非法行为的一部分，Facebook非法收购，欺凌或杀死其竞争对手，然后才可以诱骗用户并从公司的核心社会服务中牟利。据政府监管机构称，这样做实际上使Facebook几乎没有为用户提供可行的，可保护隐私的社交网络选择，他们一再援引扎克伯格自己的威胁来压制竞争对手。
   就Facebook而言，它反对这些指控，并坚持认为互联网仍然具有竞争力。该公司拒绝让高管了解这个故事，但Facebook发言人克里斯·斯格罗（Chris Sgro）在一份声明中说：“由于以下原因，我们将继续大力捍卫个人和企业选择我们的免费服务，广告和应用程序的能力：他们带来的价值。”
  此案至少与政府对技术巨头的最后一次重大反托拉斯救助具有象征性的相似之处：该公司在1990年代试图将微软分拆。州和联邦官员试图对微软采取反竞争措施，以确保其Windows操作系统和当时处于困境的个人浏览器市场在新兴的Internet Explorer Web浏览器中的统治地位，从而对微软进行惩罚。
 微软最终在阻止华盛顿和州检察长解散其业务方面占了上风，但从案件中脱颖而出，成为了一个精打细算，不那么激进的数字播放器。这次挫折实质上使像Facebook这样的硅谷新公司得以前进—几年后，这家社交网络巨头现在试图自己避免这种命运。
   “就他们所说的（特别是书面形式）以及他们如何开展业务而言，此案使微软成为一家更加谨慎的公司。我预计Facebook也将发生同样的情况。”麻省理工学院斯隆管理学院（MIT Sloan School of Management）的顶级教授，一本有关微软的书的作者迈克尔·库苏马诺（Michael A. Cusumano）说。
 对于Facebook而言，其战斗准备工作早在本月州和联邦监管机构提起诉讼之前就已经开始。 
自调查开始以来，该公司一直依靠霍华德·谢兰斯基（Howard Shelanski）的法律建议，后者曾是奥巴马政府的前高级官员，也是美国反托拉斯和数字市场的主要思想家之一。 。的确，在律师事务所Davis Polk，曾任职于美国联邦贸易委员会的经济学家，而该机构决定不阻止Facebook的收购Instagram的在2012年最近的合伙人，他曾经资助当选总统拜登2020年竞选作为外部顾问不相关的反托拉斯和监管问题。
   今年四月，Facebook还聘请了FTC前首席反托拉斯律师Barbara Blank作为公司的副总顾问。这家科技巨头最近还从国会山获得了另外两名重要助手：协助参议员戴安娜·费恩斯坦（D-Calif。）担任司法委员会委员的Anant Raut，以及为众议员Ken Buck（R-Colo）担任顾问的Ritika Robertson。 。劳特（Raut）的现任老板在烧烤扎克伯格和其他科技高管方面发挥了关键作用。罗伯逊（Robertson）的前任雇主巴克（Buck）是众议院面板上的最高共和党人，该人调查了科技巨头的反竞争行为-并建议对联邦法律进行全面修改。
 Facebook将其内部员工与大量新支出相匹配，在反托拉斯调查的有效期内投入了大量资金来影响监管机构。根据联邦伦理披露，该公司仅在联邦游说上就花费了3200万美元，这反映了2019年1月1日至2020年9月底之间的支出。
 Facebook在广告和其他政治活动上花费了额外的，未指定的金额（根据法律不必披露）。今年发起了一项名为American Edge的新运动，以捍卫技术行业不受监管审查-在美国国家步枪协会的倡导努力下，对技术结构进行建模。政府提起诉讼几天后，Facebook支持的美国边缘公司（American Edge）也进行了新的民意测验，显示美国监管机构应“保持国内科技公司的强势”。
   纽约大学法学院的反托拉斯专家哈里·First说，这种积极的反应反映了Facebook从其前辈那里学到的东西。他补充说，微软尤其缺乏政治手段，直到“为时已晚”。
 但是，First说，这家科技巨头在努力提高其首选分辨率方面面临着巨大的障碍。他说：“ Facebook的游说活动将非常艰巨，因为它的新闻报道很糟糕，扎克伯格已经获得了很多年的支持。” “归根结底，他们需要说服的人是法官。”
 夏末，FTC及其州对口单位共同罢免了扎克伯格和他的最高代表-Facebook的首席运营官雪莉·桑德伯格（Sheryl Sandberg）。罢免他的决定标志着FTC面临着重要的批评，因为它未能就2019年完成的隐私调查质疑他。 
在最近几天，Facebook高管公开地试图淡化对工人的威胁，并保证即使面对最大的政治和监管威胁，该公司也不会放慢脚步。该公司和s可亲的首席产品官克里斯·考克斯（Chris Cox）上周聚集了员工到市政厅，草拟了公司未来10年的路线图，从而挫败了Facebook即将发布的一些公告。
   新产品包括未发布的产品，该产品将使创作者付费以提供个性化的视频消息，这似乎是流行的Cameo应用程序的模仿者。考克斯说，Facebook完全致力于其古老的计划，以重新设计其通信服务（Facebook自己的消息收发应用程序以及WhatsApp和Instagram）的连线，以便一个应用程序上的用户可以与另一个应用程序上的用户进行通话。华盛顿邮报。通过整合这些技术，Facebook可能会在试图将公司分崩离析时加大对政府的负担。
 在诉讼之前，Facebook开始对其内部结构进行修补，并在全公司范围内进行重组，以解决竞争问题。 10月，它还引入了强制性的“竞争”培训，该培训指导员工如何避免监管者认为有问题的行为。员工被告知，例如，避免串通挖泥人的天才，或以可能损害竞争对手的方式贬低Facebook第三方开发人员的产品和工具。
 根据《邮政》（The Post）审查的文件，还警告员工，未经法律许可，切勿禁止开发人员访问关键的Facebook软件工具（称为API）。新的限制似乎是对州和联邦指控的特定反应，该指控指的是Facebook限制了该数据访问权限以伤害或收购潜在的竞争对手，实质上是为其服务提供武器以确保其优势。
   Facebook高管警告员工不要以书面形式讨论敏感问题，这是对扎克伯格本人公开表示要压垮竞争对手的大量电子邮件和其他信件的一点点致意。培训的截止日期是12月11日，是州和联邦官员提起诉讼的两天，促使扎克伯格本人保证“在法庭上与之抗争”。
 他在周三发送给员工的一条消息中对员工说：“今天的新闻是整个过程中的一个步骤，要完全发挥作用可能要花费数年的时间。”
note_en:
State and federal investigators were putting the finishing touches on  two landmark antitrust lawsuits against Facebook when the tech giant came to them with an early peace offering: It would help stand up its next competitor.
  For months, watchdogs in Washington and around the country had been probing Facebook and its chief executive, Mark Zuckerberg, over allegations that the company had become an illegal monopoly. Regulators had grown increasingly convinced that the social-networking behemoth over its nearly 17-year history systematically had sought to acquire or eliminate all of its rivals, engaging in unlawful tactics to become one of the most profitable digital services in the world.
 Facebook had long disputed the claims. In an attempt to illustrate its commitment to competition, the company’s top lawyers signaled that they would be open to changing some of its business practices, according to three people familiar with the matter. One of the ideas Facebook floated would have allowed another firm or developer to license access to its powerful code — and its users’ intricate web of relationships — so that they could more easily create their own version of a social network, said the individuals, who spoke on the condition of anonymity to describe a nonpublic law-enforcement inquiry.
   But the investigators ultimately rejected the idea, believing Facebook’s proposal — part of a vague early menu of remedies the tech giant presented this year — failed to fully address their competition concerns. In the end, the Federal Trade Commission and nearly every state’s attorney general, led by New York’s Letitia James, filed twin lawsuits this month that seek to break Facebook apart.
 Facebook’s tense last-minute negotiations in the final days of the state and federal probes illustrate the urgency with which the company has sought to rebuff the government’s charges — and foreshadow the aggressive tactics it is expected to deploy in the fight to come. Over the more than 18-month inquiry, Facebook ramped up its lobbying, hired high-powered former government antitrust lawyers and launched a slew of internal initiatives focused on competition issues, according to a dozen individuals, who spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss a matter in active litigation.
 Facebook’s efforts now set the stage for one of the most consequential legal clashes in the digital age: a courtroom showdown between the roughly $800 billion technology behemoth and some of the most powerful regulators in the United States. The result could shape not only the future of Facebook but the whole of Silicon Valley — and the government’s ability to police it.
   “The enforcers are shaking off decades of complacency that have given these companies a free pass,” said Democratic Sen. Richard Blumenthal, who served as Connecticut’s attorney general for 20 years before arriving in Congress a decade ago.
  The  antitrust lawsuits chiefly take aim at Facebook’s acquisition of two then-nascent social services: Instagram, a photo-sharing app, and WhatsApp, a messaging tool. Facebook bought the companies in 2012 and 2014, respectively, in multibillion-dollar deals that the FTC reviewed and did not block at the time.
 Since then, state and federal antitrust enforcers contend that these transactions ultimately became part of a pattern of troubling, illegal behavior by Facebook to  buy, bully or kill its rivals before they could siphon users and profits from the company’s core social service. In doing so, Facebook essentially has left users with few viable, privacy-protective social-networking alternatives, according to government watchdogs, who repeatedly cite Zuckerberg’s own threats to quash competitors in their complaints.
   Facebook, for its part, objects to the charges and maintains that the Internet remains competitive. The company declined to make executives available for this story, but Chris Sgro, a spokesman for Facebook, said in a statement: “We will continue to vigorously defend the ability of people and businesses to choose our free services, advertising, and apps because of the value they bring.”
  The case bears at least symbolic resemblance to the government’s last major antitrust salvo against a technology giant: its bid to break apart Microsoft in the 1990s. State and federal officials sought to penalize  Microsoft at the time for acting anti-competitively to secure the dominance of its Windows operating system and its then-fledgling Internet Explorer Web browser in the lucrative market for personal computers.
 Microsoft ultimately prevailed in preventing Washington and state attorneys general from disassembling its operations, but it emerged from the case a chastened, less aggressive digital player. The setback essentially allowed new Silicon Valley companies like Facebook to pull ahead — a fate that the social-networking giant, years later, now seeks to avoid for itself.
   “The case turned Microsoft into a more cautious company, in terms of what they would say, particularly in writing, and how they would conduct business. I expect the same is already happening with Facebook,” said Michael A. Cusumano, a top professor at the MIT Sloan School of Management and author of a book about Microsoft.
 For Facebook, its battle preparations began long before state and federal watchdogs filed their lawsuits this month.
 Since the probes began, the company has relied on legal advice from  Howard Shelanski, a former top official in the Obama administration and one of the country’s leading thinkers on antitrust and digital markets. Shelanski, a partner at the law firm Davis Polk, previously served as an economist at the FTC while the agency opted not to block Facebook’s purchase of Instagram in 2012. More recently, he has aided President-elect Joe Biden’s 2020 campaign as an outside adviser on unrelated antitrust and regulatory issues.
   This April, Facebook also hired Barbara Blank, a former top antitrust attorney at the FTC, as the company’s associate general counsel. And the tech giant recently snagged two additional critical aides from Capitol Hill: Anant Raut, who aided Sen. Dianne Feinstein (D-Calif.) on the Judiciary Committee, and Ritika Robertson, who had advised Rep. Ken Buck (R-Colo). Raut’s now-former boss has played a pivotal role in grilling Zuckerberg and other tech executives. Robertson’s past employer, Buck, is the top Republican on the House panel that investigated tech giants for their anticompetitive practices — and  recommended sweeping changes to federal law.
 Facebook matched its internal hires with a flurry of fresh spending, putting up significant sums to influence regulators over the life of the antitrust probe. The company has shelled out $32 million on federal lobbying alone,  according to federal ethics disclosures, which reflect spending between Jan. 1, 2019, and the end of September 2020.
 Facebook spent additional, unspecified amounts, which do not have to be disclosed under law, on advertising and other political initiatives. One new campaign, called American Edge,  launched this year to defend the tech industry from regulatory scrutiny — modeling its structure after the advocacy efforts of the National Rifle Association. Days after the government filed its lawsuits, the Facebook-backed American Edge also commissioned new polling showing that U.S. regulators should “keep domestic tech companies strong.”
   The aggressive response reflects Facebook’s learnings from its predecessors, said Harry First, an antitrust expert at New York University School of Law, who added that Microsoft in particular had lacked a political apparatus until it was “too late.”
 But, First said, the tech giant faces immense obstacles in trying to muscle in its preferred resolution. “Facebook’s lobbying campaign will be tough because of the terrible press it and Zuckerberg have been getting for a number of years,” he said. “At the end of the day, the person they will need to convince is a judge.”
 The FTC and its state counterparts jointly deposed Zuckerberg as well as his top deputy — Sheryl Sandberg, Facebook’s chief operating officer — over videoconferencing software in late summer. The decision to depose him marked an important turn for the FTC after it  faced immense criticism for failing to question him in connection with the privacy probe it concluded in 2019.
  Publicly, Facebook executives in recent days have sought to downplay the threat to their workers, pledging that the company would not slow down even in the face of the greatest political and regulatory threat it has faced. Chris Cox, the company’s affable chief product officer, rattled off some of Facebook’s upcoming announcements when he gathered employees last week for a town hall sketching out the company’s road map for the next 10 years.
   The new offerings included an unreleased product that would pay creators to give personalized video messages, seemingly a copycat of the popular  Cameo app. And Cox said Facebook is fully committed to its years-old plan to rewire its communications services — Facebook’s own messaging app, as well as WhatsApp and Instagram — so that users on one app can talk to those on another, according to audio obtained by The Washington Post. By integrating these technologies, Facebook may make it harder on the government as it seeks to break the company apart.
 Before the lawsuit, Facebook began to tinker with its internal structure, instituting a companywide reorganization to focus on competition issues. In October, it also introduced mandatory “competition” training that instructed employees about ways to avoid behaviors that regulators might consider problematic. Employees were told to avoid colluding to poach talent, for example, or deprecating the products and tools of Facebook’s third-party developers in a way that might harm those rivals.
 Employees also were warned not to cut off developers from access to critical Facebook software tools, known as APIs, without legal approval, according to documents reviewed by The Post. The new restriction appeared to be a specific response to state and federal allegations that Facebook had limited this data access to hurt or acquire potential competitors, essentially weaponizing its service to ensure its dominance.
   And Facebook executives cautioned employees not to discuss sensitive issues in writing, in a subtle nod to the reams of emails and other correspondence from Zuckerberg himself that spoke openly about crushing his competition. The deadline for the training was Dec. 11, two days after state and federal officials would file their case, prompting Zuckerberg himself to pledge to “fight this in court.”
 “Today’s news is one step in a process,” he told employees in a message sent that Wednesday, “which could take years to play out in its entirety.”
title: 资料来源：在美国反托拉斯调查的早期阶段，Facebook提出使用其网络和代码来帮助其他人建立社交网络竞争对手。
title_en: Sources: in the early stages of US antitrust investigations, Facebook offered to help others build a social networking competitor using its network and code

路透社周一报道称，苹果的目标是最快在2024年为消费者生产苹果品牌的汽车。
 这增加了两家制造激光雷达传感器的公司的库存，激光雷达传感器是自动驾驶汽车的核心组件，可以使他们的计算机拍摄周围世界的3D图像。
 路透社报道称，苹果公司计划最早在2024年为消费者生产苹果品牌的汽车。周一，两家生产激光雷达传感器的公司的股票飙升。
   激光雷达是自动驾驶汽车的核心组件，可让其计算机拍摄周围世界的3D图像。传感器使用类似于雷达的过程来测量物体的范围或速度-它们从物体上反射激光，当光返回时，传感器可以计算出物体有多远以及物体是否在移动。
 无人驾驶汽车公司如Alphabet的Waymo和通用汽车的Cruise都在其自动驾驶系统中大量使用了激光雷达。该报告称，苹果决定将外部合作伙伴用于自动驾驶系统的一部分，包括激光雷达传感器。
 但是，该报告并未提及苹果的任何潜在激光雷达供应商，并且还指出，苹果可以决定使用其设计的激光雷达传感器。较新的iPhone和iPad在其摄像头模块中配备了Apple设计的激光雷达传感器，尽管该传感器可能需要进行改装才能在车辆上使用。
  苹果已经在加利福尼亚州对自动驾驶汽车进行了测试，方向盘后面有一个可以接管的安全驾驶员。这些车辆的早期版本在顶部安装了可见的激光雷达传感器，但随后在加利福尼亚街头发现的激光雷达传感器则将其隐藏在机壳内。苹果公司还运营着一批车辆，可为苹果地图捕获街道级图像，并且这些车还配备了激光雷达传感器。 
最近围绕电动和自动驾驶汽车的热潮推动了该行业的股价上涨。 截至周一收盘，特斯拉的股价今年迄今为止上涨了655％。  Velodyne于9月通过SPAC上市。 当时，它表示其在2019年的收入为1.014亿美元，净亏损为6720万美元。11月，它表示在最近一个季度出售了2235个传感器单元，平均售价为5600美元。  Luminar本月初通过SPAC公开上市，此后增长了172％。 它是一家专注于技术开发的较小公司，在2019年亏损了1,470万美元，亏损了9,470万美元。
note_en:
Reuters reported on Monday that Apple is aiming to start production of an Apple-branded car for consumers as soon as 2024.
 That boosted the stocks of two companies that make lidar sensors, a core component for self-driving cars that allows their computers to take a 3D image of the world around them.
 Two companies that make lidar sensors saw their stocks soar on Monday after Reuters  reported on hat Apple is aiming to start production of an Apple-branded car for consumers as soon as 2024.
   Lidar is a core component for self-driving cars that allows their computers to take a 3D image of the world around them. The sensors measure the range or velocity of objects using a process that is similar to radar -- they bounce lasers off objects and when the light returns, the sensors can calculate how far away the object is and if it's moving.
 Self-driving car companies like  Alphabet's Waymo and  GM's Cruise make heavy use of lidar in their self-driving systems. Apple has decided to use outside partners for parts of its self-driving system, including lidar sensors, according to the report.
 However, the report does not name any prospective lidar suppliers to Apple, and also also notes that Apple could instead decide to use lidar sensors that it designed. Newer iPhones and iPads come with an Apple-designed lidar sensor in their camera modules, although this sensor would likely have to be modified for use on a vehicle.
  Apple has tested self-driving cars in California with a safety driver behind the wheel who can take over. An early version of those vehicles had visible lidar sensors mounted on the top, but  a subsequent version spotted on California streets hid the sensors inside a casing. Apple also runs a fleet of vehicles that capture street-level imagery for Apple Maps, and those cars are  also equipped with lidar sensors.
  A recent wave of excitement around electric and self-driving cars has driven up stock prices in the sector.  Tesla's stock is up 655% year-to-date, as of Monday's close.
  Velodyne went public through a SPAC in September. At the time, it said it recorded a net loss of $67.2 million on revenue of $101.4 million in 2019. In November,  it said it sold 2,235 sensor units in the most recent quarter, at an average selling price of $5,600.
 Luminar went public through a SPAC earlier this month, and it's up over 172% since then. It's a smaller company focused on developing technology, and  lost $94.7 million in 2019 on revenue of $12.6 million.
title: 苹果在汽车上工作

周一，美国商务部公布了中俄两国有军事联系的公司名单。该列表将58家中国公司和43家俄罗斯公司指定为“军事最终用户”。并要求出口商在销售产品之前获得许可证。此类许可证不太可能颁发。
 “国防部认识到利用与美国和全球公司的伙伴关系以打击中国和俄罗斯为破坏美国军事计划而动用美国技术的重要性，这很重要，＆＃34;商务部长威尔伯·罗斯在宣布这一消息时表示。
  该名单包括中国和俄罗斯的多家航天公司，包括俄罗斯萨马拉的Progress Rocket航天中心。该公司开发和制造联盟号火箭，在美国航天飞机退役后的最近十年中，该火箭一直将俄罗斯和美国宇航员带到国际空间站。
 联盟制造商的加入引起了俄罗斯航天公司负责人罗斯科斯莫斯（Roscosmos）领导人德米特里·罗戈津（Dmitry Rogozin）的迅速谴责。罗戈津在激烈的声明中说，这些限制是“非法的”。他把它们描述为“愚蠢”。
 ＆＃34;这家Samara企业生产了具有传奇色彩的Soyuz-2运载火箭，在这种帮助下，Soyuz MS航天器已经将美国宇航员带到ISS已有10年了，＆＃34;他说。 ＆＃34;现在，事实证明，我们的美国同事再次进行了“蹦床工作”，他们所做的第一件事就是将萨马拉井吐出来。同事，是否为时过早，以防万一您的“蹦床”突然断裂，您将不得不再次满足我们对太空的热情？＆＃34;
     罗戈津（Rogozin）对蹦床的评论要追溯到6年前，当时他本人是在俄罗斯入侵克里米亚之后遭到美国政府制裁的。当时，当美国国家航空航天局完全注视俄罗斯以将宇航员带到国际空间站时，罗戈津说，在分析了对我们航天工业的制裁之后，我建议美国用蹦床将宇航员运送到国际空间站。 ＃34;
 最终，这让罗戈津感到尴尬。今年5月，自2011年以来，SpaceX的“乘员龙”号和“猎鹰9号”火箭首次从美国将人类送入轨道后，公司创始人埃隆·马斯克开玩笑说，蹦床正在工作。自那时以来，SpaceX已安全地向太空站发射了第二名机组人员，并计划在2021年再执行两次飞行任务。Roscosmos尚未承诺在美国飞行器上驾驶宇航员。 
然后，罗戈津（Rogozin）在他的最新评论中暗示SpaceX运载工具可能还存在问题。 正如Ars最近报道的那样，这也许不足为奇，Rogozin似乎痴迷于SpaceX的成功以及它如何损害了自己公司的经济前景。  这些似乎都无助于NASA和Roscosmos之间正在进行的空间站合作。 罗戈津对此表示认同，并说，这些制裁是有害的，因为它们将在俄美两国在太空，特别是在国际空间站之间的这种重要合作中制造更多的障碍和烦恼。  六年前，罗戈津（Rogozin）的话语最终被证明只是b不休。 今天的声明和对这些经济限制的影响是否会导致国际伙伴关系出现更永久的问题，这种伙伴关系已经成功地在空间站飞行了二十多年。

    该公司安全政策负责人纳撒尼尔·格莱歇（Nathaniel Gleicher）告诉Axios，Facebook明年计划为希望采取额外措施保护其帐户的用户提供对安全令牌的移动支持。
 推动新闻：Facebook还将在明年将其Facebook Protect安全计划扩展到更多类型的帐户。该计划将提供给新闻工作者，人权捍卫者和名人等弱势用户，以及即将举行大选国家的用户。
 Facebook Protect包含其他安全措施，例如两因素身份验证和对潜在黑客威胁的实时监控。
 迄今为止，只有在美国，政客，政党官员，政府机构，选举工作人员以及所有参与选举过程并带有经过蓝色徽章验证的Facebook页面的人都可以使用它。
 建议为高调帐户使用安全密钥，但是任何想要一个的Facebook帐户所有者均可使用该安全密钥。
 用户将可以亲自和在线从各种零售商处购买代币，然后可以在Facebook上进行注册。 
是的，但是：虽然硬件密钥是经过时间考验的安全控制，但它们并非一成不变，并且可能会丢失或被盗。
 因此，Gleicher建议拥有高知名度帐户的用户同时使用Facebook Protect和安全密钥。
 ＆＃34;坏演员正试图锁定具有突出声音的社交媒体资产。仅仅因为您不是首席执行官或政治候选人，并不意味着您不是您所在领域的杰出人物和目标，他说。
 总体情况：Facebook认为，自2016年以来的最大改进之一是，它阻止不良行为者入侵真实帐户或创建虚假帐户以传播虚假信息。这使Facebook的错误信息问题从2016年的安全问题转变为2020年的更多内容审核问题。
 ＆＃34;我们的论点是，您必须保护帐户，因为每一个被盗用的资产都可能成为不良行为者用来在以后造成更大伤害（甚至更大）的工具，除了对人造成直接伤害之外，＆＃34;格莱歇尔说。
 根据数字：2020年，Facebook估计与美国大选密切相关的人中有70％以上使用了两因素身份验证。
 Facebook促使许多用户（例如州和选举官员，候选人和政党领袖）签署了该功能，但它最终还是依赖于接近选举工作的人们选择加入该程序。 
下一步：除了这些安全保护措施之外，Facebook表示还将扩大对安全威胁的公开报告。 Gleicher说，公开披露不同的影响力行动或黑客企图有助于阻止和减慢不良行为者。

    Facebook next year plans to roll out mobile support for security tokens for users who want to take extra measures protect their accounts, the company's head of security policy Nathaniel Gleicher tells Axios.
 Driving the news: Facebook will also expand its Facebook Protect security program to more types of accounts next year. The program will be made available to vulnerable users like journalists, human rights defenders and celebrities, and also to users in countries with upcoming major elections.
 Facebook Protect includes additional security provisions, like two-factor authentication and real-time monitoring for potential hacking threats.
 To-date, it has only been available in the U.S. to politicians, party officials, government agencies, election staff and anyone with a blue-badge verified Facebook page who is involved in the election process.
 The security keys are advised for high-profile accounts, but will available for use to any Facebook account holder that wants one.
 Users will be able to buy the tokens from various retailers in-person and online, and then will be able to register them with Facebook.
 Yes, but: While hardware keys are a time-tested security control, they're not invulnerable, and they can be lost or stolen.
 It's for this reason that Gleicher recommends users with high-profile accounts use both Facebook Protect and security keys.
 "Bad actors are trying to target social media assets of prominent voices. Just because you're not a CEO or a political candidate doesn't mean you're not a prominent person in your field and a target," he says.
 The big picture: Facebook believes one of its biggest improvements from 2016 was that it blocked bad actors from hacking real accounts or creating fake accounts to spread disinformation. This transformed Facebook's misinformation problem from being a security matter in 2016 to more of a content moderation issue in 2020.
 "Our thesis is that you have to protect accounts because every compromised asset can become a tool that is used by bad actors for greater harm — much greater — afterwards, in addition to causing immediate harm to people," says Gleicher.
 By the numbers: In 2020, Facebook estimates that over 70% of people who were closely involved with the U.S. election turned on two-factor authentication.
 While Facebook prompted many users, like state and election officials, candidates, and party leaders to sign up for the feature, it ultimately relied on people working close to the election to opt in to the program.
 What's next: In addition to these security protections, Facebook says it will also expand its public reporting of security threats. The public disclosures of different influence operations or hack attempts help to deter and slow down bad actors, Gleicher says.
title: Facebook宣布明年推出新的安全工具：对安全密钥的移动支持，Facebook Protect程序的扩展等
title_en: Facebook announces new security tools rolling out next year: mobile support for security keys, expansion of the Facebook Protect program, and more

     随着大流行把学校推向虚拟教室，许多软件供应商已经加紧提供最新的监视工具。像Proctorio这样的公司提供的技术声称可以通过跟踪头部和眼睛的运动来对抗作弊行为，而没有任何证据表明他们的算法除了会使学生焦虑（从而表现得更差）之外，还可以起到其他作用。其他人依靠面部识别技术，该技术本身充斥着种族偏见，并且在参加状态栏考试时经常未能在各个点上验证有色学生的身份，从而迫使测试结束。
    Proctorio是受到隐私小组审查的少数公司之一，这些公司不仅进行侵入性监视，而且还进行详尽的数据提取，收集包括生物特征识别在内的敏感学生数据。该公司在试图使其监视程序的批评者保持沉默方面可能是独特的。 Proctorio已展开诉讼，以使批评家保持沉默，由于不愿提起其对公司的在线批评，该诉讼迫使不列颠哥伦比亚大学的一名学习技术专家用尽了个人和紧急储蓄。 Proctorio还针对学生，并滥用了Twitter的DMCA删除流程，以进一步制止对其监理软件的有效批评。
     ＆＃34;自动监理也是对我们孩子隐私的直接和可恶的侵犯。 Proctorio和其他公司可以从我们的孩子那里访问他们的个人数据，包括他们的个人计算机，家里的私人房间以及其他数据。”这封信是由“为未来而奋斗”与“父母一起”组织的父母签署的， 250万个上级组织。 “我们的孩子必须放弃其公民权利，尤其是在进入公共机构时，才能完成其教育，这是令人无法接受的＆＃34;

（路透社）-包括微软和谷歌在内的科技巨头周一加入了Facebook与黑客组织NSO的法律斗争，在联邦法院提交了一份法庭之友文件，警告以色列公司的工具“功能强大且危险”。
 该摘要提交给了美国第九巡回上诉法院，该摘要在Facebook针对NSO提起的诉讼中开辟了一条新的战线。该摘要于去年披露，因为该网络监视公司利用了Facebook拥有的即时消息中的一个错误。计划WhatsApp可以帮助全球1400多人进行监视。
  NSO辩称，因为它向警察和间谍机构出售数字入侵工具，所以它应该从“主权豁免权”中受益。“主权豁免权”是一种法律学说，通常使外国政府免受诉讼之害。 NSO于7月在加利福尼亚北部地区失去了这一论点，此后呼吁第九巡回法院推翻该裁决。
 微软，谷歌，谷歌，思科，戴尔技术公司所属的VMWare以及总部位于华盛顿的互联网协会联合微软与Facebook争辩说，对NSO授予主权豁免将导致黑客技术的扩散，“更多外国拥有强大而危险的网络监视工具的政府。”
 简报认为，这反过来“意味着这些工具落入错误手中并被恶意使用的机会大大增加”。
 NSO并没有立即返回征求意见的消息，称其产品被用于打击犯罪。但是，位于多伦多的Citizen Lab和位于伦敦的国际特赦组织等地方的人权捍卫者和技术专家已经记录了一些案例，其中NSO技术已被用来瞄准记者，律师甚至是游说苏打税的营养学家。
 公民实验室周日发表报告称，已经部署了NSO的电话黑客技术，以入侵位于卡塔尔广播公司Al Jazeera的记者，制片人，主持人和高管的三打手机，以及位于伦敦的Al Araby电视台。 
NSO的间谍软件还与华盛顿邮报记者贾马尔·卡舒吉（Jamal Khashoggi）遭到杀害有关，后者于2018年在伊斯坦布尔的沙特领事馆被谋杀并被肢解。 查看导致他死亡的WhatsApp消息。  NSO否认了对Khashoggi的黑客攻击，但迄今为止，拒绝评论其技术是否曾被用来监视他圈子中的其他人。

( Reuters) — Tech giants, including Microsoft and Google, on Monday joined Facebook’s legal battle against hacking company  NSO, filing an amicus brief in federal court that warned the Israeli firm’s tools were “powerful, and dangerous.”
 The brief, filed before the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit, opens up a new front in Facebook’s lawsuit against NSO,  which it filed last year after it was revealed that the cyber surveillance firm had exploited a bug in Facebook-owned instant messaging program WhatsApp to help surveil more than 1,400 people worldwide.
  NSO has argued that because it sells digital break-in tools to police and spy agencies, it should benefit from “sovereign immunity” — a legal doctrine that generally insulates foreign governments from lawsuits. NSO lost that argument in the Northern District of California in July and has since appealed to the Ninth Circuit to have the ruling overturned.
 Microsoft, Alphabet-owned Google, Cisco, Dell Technologies-owned VMWare, and the Washington-based Internet Association joined forces with Facebook to argue against that, saying that awarding sovereign immunity to NSO would lead to a proliferation of hacking technology and “more foreign governments with powerful and dangerous cyber surveillance tools.”
 That in turn “means dramatically more opportunities for those tools to fall into the wrong hands and be used nefariously,” the brief argues.
 NSO — which did not immediately return a message seeking comment — argues that its products are used to fight crime. But human rights defenders and technologists at places such as Toronto-based Citizen Lab and London-based Amnesty International have documented cases in which NSO technology has been used to target reporters, lawyers, and even nutritionists lobbying for soda taxes.
 Citizen Lab published a report on Sunday alleging that NSO’s phone-hacking technology had been deployed to hack three dozen phones belonging to journalists, producers, anchors, and executives at Qatar-based broadcaster Al Jazeera, as well as a device belonging to a reporter at London-based Al Araby TV.
 NSO’s spyware has also been linked to the slaying of  Washington Post journalist Jamal Khashoggi, who was murdered and dismembered in the Saudi consulate in Istanbul in 2018. Khashoggi’s friend, dissident video blogger Omar Abdulaziz, has long argued that it was the Saudi government’s ability to see their WhatsApp messages that led to his death.
 NSO has denied hacking Khashoggi but has so far declined to comment on whether its technology was used to spy on others in his circle.
title: 微软和谷歌加入FB与以色列黑客公司NSO的法律斗争
title_en: Microsoft and Google join FB's legal battle against Israeli hacking company NSO

2008年1月15日，史蒂夫·乔布斯（Steve Jobs）从一个马尼拉信封中取出MacBook Air，并永远更换了笔记本电脑。眨眼，您可能会错过它：整个行业转移以容纳对更多人更有意义的新型设备的那一刻。上个月，苹果敢于再做一次。花了12年零10个月，但苹果终于有了一个新的“游戏规则改变者”。不是增量更新。不是供粉丝使用的计算机。但是，真正的游戏规则改变者将迫使业界再次进行重新配置。
 Apple Silicon Macs是一种新型设备。因为基于ARM的计算机不是新事物；直到一个月前，高性能，超快速，超高效的基于ARM的计算机还是一个空想。如果基于ARM的Windows机器代表陆地上一条变异鱼类的缓慢而缓慢的第一步，那么苹果公司已经创造出了成熟的陆地哺乳动物。
    苹果上个月发布了三台Apple Silicon Macs，它们均以Apple M1 SOC为核心。现在有M1 MacBook Air完全无风扇，M1 MacBook Pro拥有据报道的20个小时的苹果电池续航时间，还有M1 Mac Mini，这三人中的一台不必担心自己的计算机电池寿命。
   额外的1TB硬盘空间增加了价格。如果我为摄影师推荐一种配置，我会告诉您让这台计算机只有1TB的内置存储空间，而不是2台，这样总价格会降到1,900美元。
   在外观上，M1 MacBook Pro与我们今年早些时候评测的13英寸基于Intel的机器几乎没有区别。相同的机箱，相同的键盘，相同的触控板，相同的指纹传感器，相同的网络摄像头（叹气），相同的触控栏（meh），没有SD卡插槽（ugh）。
 这两款计算机在外面唯一的区别是Thunderbolt 3端口：高端Intel Macs仍带有4个端口，而新的Apple Silicon Macs仅具有2个端口。对于某些人来说，这可能是一个大问题，但是，如果是这样，您只需等待。随着Apple Silicon的进一步发展，M（x）处理器的未来迭代中肯定会有更多的端口。
 除了缺少端口外，该计算机与其前身相同。新的“魔术”键盘是市场上最好的键盘之一，触摸板仍然是笔记本电脑上响应速度最快的键盘，并且显示屏明亮，色彩准确。根据我们使用DataColor Spyder X Elite进行的测量，您可以预期Display P3色域的100％覆盖率和AdobeRGB的89％覆盖率。 
总结：在外面，一切照旧。没什么大变化，虽然我很希望看到苹果利用这次机会来展示具有较小屏幕边框和更大电池的神话般的14英寸MacBook Pro，但无论从什么意义上讲，当前的设计和制造质量仍然是“溢价” ，即使它看起来有点过时了。
  使这台计算机如此特别的所有东西都藏在里面。 Apple Silicon M1片上系统（SOC）与Apple的一些出色软件相结合，使新款MacBook Pro能够完成我所拥有的基于Intel的笔记本电脑无法做到的事情。
 风扇几乎永远不会打开，机箱在最极端的基准测试中保持凉爽的触感，而且我们使用的每个应用程序（针对Apple Silicon进行了优化或由Apple的Rosetta 2软件进行了实时仿真）的性能均优于我们预期。 Lightroom Classic尤其如此，它似乎一点也不挣扎。
  引用一位为自己购买了新M1 Mac Mini的技术编辑的话：
 老实说，我已经没有办法测试其极限。我已经将EOS R中的10位ProRes 4K视频（从HDMI录制到Ninja V）进行了转码，将成千上万的图像导入Lightroom Classic（显然仍然通过Rosetta 2运行），并运行我能想到的所有基准测试工具。这东西不引人注目。除非我找到从Red获取一些8K Raw视频的方法，否则我不确定能否看到这些限制。
 我们自己的Lightroom Classic和Photoshop基准测试2有助于量化这种性能。尽管Adobe的应用程序具有足够的能力对任何距离内的计算机进行破坏，但Apple Silicon Mac却像冠军一样处理了我们的测试。下图显示了针对基于Intel的2020年13英寸MacBook Pro（2.3GhZ四核i7、32GB RAM）和M1 MacBook Pro的标准Lightroom Import，Lightroom Export和PugetBench基准测试结果，其中新的Dell XPS 17作为高端参考点。
 请记住，在所有这些测试中，M1 MacBook均使用Rosetta 2来模拟Lightroom Classic和Photoshop 2021 3。 
如您所见，在所有Lightroom基准测试中，M1 Mac都击败了Intel Mac。尽管它在Pugetbench中仍然输了，但利润并不是很大。所有这一切都与售价700美元以上且具有2倍RAM 4的英特尔MacBook Pro相比。如果这是M1芯片通过仿真运行Lightroom Classic和Photoshop的能力，那么经过完全优化的版本绝对可以在使用一小部分电源的情况下运行。
 即使谨慎的图形卡和额外的内核在PugetBench中也有很大的不同，即使在Dell中，戴尔在Lightroom中也没有表现出比M1 MBP更好的性能。
 最后，这将我们带到了电池续航时间。为了查看M1 MacBook Pro与基于Intel的版本相比，我进行了一个简单的测试。我打开了Google Chrome浏览器，并在YouTube上排队了一个非常长的4K视频，在后台的另一个窗口中打开了5个随机标签（主要是“严重饮食食谱”）。然后，我将视频全屏播放，直到耗尽电池为止。
 Intel Mac在5小时27分钟内完全耗尽了电量，这应该让您了解Chrome的耗电量。 M1 Mac？历时15小时50分钟。
 请记住，这些机器在其他方面是相同的。实际上，M1 MacBook Pro是使用基于Intel版本的Time Machine备份设置的。两者都运行相同的软件，相同的后台进程，并且在点击YouTube视频上的“播放”之前，都已充满电并拔下电源。
 15小时50秒…在CHROME中。在正常使用下，我经常能够完成两整天的工作，而无需插电。无论我多么轻易地使用它，我都无法对其他任何计算机这么说。仅从效率来看，这将改变游戏规则；再加上上述空前的仿真和性能指标，您实际上需要引起注意。
   苹果公司比任何人都预料到的这些第一代Apple Silicon设备要领先得多。 Rosetta 2仿真非常好，几乎看不见，性能大大超过了价格点（至少按照Apple标准），效率绝对令人赞叹。我希望对基于ARM的应用程序“好”，对未优化的应用程序“可用”。我没想到在1/4的功耗和3/4的价格下性能会有所改善。 
推荐第一代产品总是很困难。新生技术的最初几次迭代通常会带来最积极的改进，如果M1已经很好，我就迫不及待想看看M1X或M2带来了什么。令人惊讶的是，苹果公司成功消除了其他一切待命原因。
 M1 Macs不是“早期采用者”技术。这些产品已经准备好黄金时间。每个主要的创意软件开发人员都已经优先考虑对Apple Silicon的全面支持，Rosetta 2在此期间可以照顾您，并且电池寿命的增加使M1笔记本电脑比Intel替代产品更容易选择。
 但是，无论您是立即需要一台新Mac还是愿意等待看看Apple在iMac或下一代16英寸MacBook Pro中保留了什么，我可以毫不犹豫地说一件事：这改变了一切。
  作者简介：DL Cade是一位艺术，科学和技术作家，曾任PetaPixel总编辑。当他不写文章或评论最新的创意技术时，您会发现他在华盛顿大学的视觉科学学院工作，每周出版《三点新闻》通讯或在Medium上分享个人文章。
    使用100个61MP Sony a7R IV文件和150个100MP PhaseOne XF文件运行2个标准的Lightroom导入和导出测试。相机RAW缓存被清除，程序在两次到达运行之间重新启动。每个报告的时间至少基于3次连续运行。对于Photoshop测试，我们使用Puget Systems的PugetBench v0.8，因为它是具有照片合并测试功能的最新版本。所有分数均基于至少5次连续运行。 ↩
 3我们很想在Photoshop for ARM的beta版本上运行Photoshop基准测试，该版本在撰写本文时已可用，但是beta版本仍缺少PugetBench所需的某些功能。 ↩
 4台配备2.3GHz四核i7、32GB RAM和2TB SSD的13英寸MacBook Pro售价2,600美元。配备1TB SSD的顶级M1 MacBook Pro售价1,900美元。
note_en:
On January 15th, 2008, Steve Jobs pulled a MacBook Air out of a manila envelope and changed laptop computers forever. Blink and you might have missed it: the instant when an entire industry shifted to accommodate a new breed of device that made more sense to more people. And last month, Apple had the audacity to do it again.  It took 12 years and 10 months, but Apple finally has a new “game-changer.” Not an incremental update. Not a computer for fanboys. But a true game-changer that will force the industry to re-configure yet again.
 The Apple Silicon Macs are a new breed of device. Because while ARM-based computers are not new; highly functional, blazing-fast, ultra-efficient ARM-based computers were a pipe dream until about a month ago. If ARM-based Windows machines represent the first slow, plodding steps of a mutant fish on land, Apple has created a full-blown land mammal.
    Apple released three Apple Silicon Macs last month, all of them featuring the Apple M1 SOC at their core. There’s the M1 MacBook Air that is now completely fanless, the M1 MacBook Pro which boasts a reported 20 hours of battery life in Apple’s metrics, and the M1 Mac Mini, the one computer out of this trio that doesn’t have to concern itself with battery life.
   That extra 1TB of hard drive space adds a lot to the price. If I were recommending a configuration for photographers, I’d tell you to get this same computer with only 1TB of built-in storage instead of 2, which brings the total price down to $1,900.
   On the outside, there is almost nothing to distinguish an M1 MacBook Pro from the 13-inch Intel-based machine  we reviewed earlier this year. Same chassis, same keyboard, same trackpad, same fingerprint sensor, same webcam (sigh), same Touch Bar (meh), no SD card slot (ugh).
 The only thing setting these two computers apart on the outside is the Thunderbolt 3 ports: the top-end Intel Macs still come with 4 ports, while the new Apple Silicon Macs feature only two  1. This might be a deal-breaker for some, but if it is, all you have to do is wait. More ports are almost certainly coming to future iterations of the M(x) processor as Apple Silicon is developed further.
 Beyond the lack of ports, the computer is identical to its predecessor. The new “Magic” keyboard is among the best on the market, the touchpad is still the most responsive you’ll find on a laptop, and the display is bright and color accurate. Based on our measurements using a  DataColor Spyder X Elite, you can expect 100% coverage of the Display P3 gamut and 89% coverage of AdobeRGB.
   In summary: on the outside, it’s business as usual. Nothing much has changed, and while I would have loved to see Apple use this opportunity to reveal the mythical 14-inch MacBook Pro with smaller screen bezels and an even bigger battery, the current design and build quality is still “premium” by any definition, even if it’s starting to look a bit dated.
  Everything that makes this computer so special is tucked away inside. The Apple Silicon M1 System on a Chip (SOC), combined with some brilliant software from Apple, allows the new MacBook Pro to do things that no Intel-based laptop I’ve ever owned could do.
 The fans almost  never turn on, the chassis stays cool to the touch during the most extreme benchmarks, and every app we used—weather it was optimized for Apple Silicon or being emulated in real-time by Apple’s Rosetta 2 software—performed better than we expected. This was particularly true of Lightroom Classic, which didn’t seem to struggle at all.
  To quote a fellow tech editor who purchased the new M1 Mac Mini for himself:
 I’m honestly running out of ways to test its limits. I’ve transcoded 10-bit ProRes 4K video from my EOS R (recorded over HDMI to Ninja V), imported thousands of images into Lightroom Classic (which is obviously still run through Rosetta 2), and run every benchmarking tool I can think of. This thing doesn’t bat an eye. Unless I can find a way to get some 8K Raw video from Red, I’m not sure I’ll be able to see the limitations.
 Our own set of Lightroom Classic and Photoshop benchmarks  2 help quantify this performance. While Adobe’s apps are more than capable of walloping any computer within walloping distance, the Apple Silicon Mac handled our tests like a champ. The charts below show the results of our standard Lightroom Import, Lightroom Export, and PugetBench benchmarks for both the Intel-based 2020 13-inch MacBook Pro (2.3GhZ Quad-Core i7, 32GB of RAM) and the M1 MacBook Pro, with the new Dell XPS 17 as a high-end reference point.
 Keep in mind that the M1 MacBook was using Rosetta 2 to emulate Lightroom Classic and Photoshop 2021  3 for all of these tests.
         As you can see, M1 Mac beats Intel Mac in  every Lightroom benchmark across the board; and while it still lost out in Pugetbench, the margin isn’t exactly huge. All this against an Intel MacBook Pro that costs $700 more and has 2x the RAM  4. If this is how well the M1 chip runs Lightroom Classic and Photoshop via emulation, fully optimized versions are absolutely going to fly while using a fraction of the power.
 Even the Dell didn’t outperform the M1 MBP by much in Lightroom, although the discreet graphics card and extra cores made a big difference in PugetBench.
 Which, finally, brings us to battery life. To see how the M1 MacBook Pro compared to the Intel-based version, I ran a simple test. I opened Google Chrome and queued up a very very long 4K video on YouTube, with 5 random tabs (mostly Serious Eats recipes) open in another window in the background. I then ran that video full screen for as long as it took to kill the battery.
 The Intel Mac drained fully in 5 hours and 27 minutes, which should give you an idea of how much of a battery hog Chrome is. The M1 Mac? It lasted 15 hours and 50 minutes.
 Keep in mind that these machines are otherwise identical. In fact, the M1 MacBook Pro was set up using a Time Machine backup of the Intel-based version. Both are running the same software, the same background processes, and both were fully charged and unplugged only moments before hitting “play” on the YouTube video.
 15 hours and 50 seconds… IN CHROME. And under normal use, I was frequently able to get two full days of work done without ever having to plug up. I have never been able to say that about any other computer, no matter how lightly I tried to use it. Judging the computer on efficiency alone, it would be a game-changer; pair that with the unprecedented emulation and the performance metrics above, and you’ve got something that practically demands your attention.
   Apple is much further ahead than anybody expected them to be with these 1st generation Apple Silicon devices. Rosetta 2 emulation is so good it’s practically invisible, performance exceeds price point by a lot (at least by Apple standards), and efficiency is absolutely mind-blowing. I hoped for “good” with the ARM-based apps, and “usable” with non-optimized apps; I did  not expect improved performance at 1/4 the power draw and 3/4 the price.
     It’s always difficult to recommend a first-generation product. The first few iterations of a nascent technology typically bring the most aggressive improvements, and if the M1 is already this good I can’t wait to see what the M1X or the M2 bring to the table. What’s surprising is that Apple has successfully eliminated  every other reason to wait around.
 The M1 Macs are not “early adopter” technology. These products are already ready for prime time. Full support for Apple Silicon is already being prioritized by every major developer of creative software, Rosetta 2 is more than capable of taking care of you in the meantime, and the battery life gains alone make the M1 laptops an easy choice over the Intel alternatives.
 But whether you need a new Mac right away or you’re willing to wait and see what Apple sticks inside the iMac or next-gen 16-inch MacBook Pro, there’s one thing I can say without any hesitation: this changes everything.
  About the author:  DL Cade is an art, science and technology writer, and the former Editor in Chief of PetaPixel. When he’s not writing op-eds or reviewing the latest tech for creatives, you’ll find him working in Vision Sciences at the University of Washington, publishing the weekly  Triple Point newsletter, or sharing personal essays on  Medium.
    2 Standard Lightroom Import and Export tests are run using 100 61MP Sony a7R IV files and 150 100MP PhaseOne XF files. The Camera RAW Cache is purged and the program is restarted between reach run. Each reported time is based on a minimum of 3 consecutive runs. For Photoshop testing, we use Puget Systems’ PugetBench v0.8, because it is the last version to feature the Photo Merge Test. All scores are based on a minimum of 5 consecutive runs. ↩
 3 We would have loved to run the Photoshop benchmark on the beta version of Photoshop for ARM, which  was available as of this writing, but the beta is still missing certain features that PugetBench requires. ↩
 4 13-inch MacBook Pro with 2.3GHz Quad Core i7, 32GB of RAM, and 2TB SSD costs $2,600. The top-of-the-line M1 MacBook Pro with 1TB of SSD costs $1,900.↩
title: Apple Silicon M1 MacBook Pro评论：这改变了一切
title_en: Apple Silicon M1 MacBook Pro Review: This Changes Everything

中国国家航空航天局说，中国的新型运载火箭“长征八号”于周二进行了首次飞行，这是部署可重复使用的运载火箭战略的第一阶段。 “长征八号”系列是中国研发可重复使用火箭的努力的一部分，这有可能降低任务成本并为商业发射服务铺平道路。
  该计划与美国私人火箭公司SpaceX的猎鹰系列具有相似之处，尽管中国在2018年表示其可重复使用的运载工具将使用不同的技术。
  新的中型运​​载火箭在星期​​二北京时间下午12:37（格林尼治标准时间0437）从海南岛南部的文昌发射场发射了五颗卫星，使其进入计划的轨道。
  它的高度为50.3米，起飞质量为356吨，中国国家航天局（CNSA）表示，这对于加速运载火箭的升级具有重大意义。
  新华社周二报道，该火箭的设计基于先前的长征版本开发的技术。
  Long March-8的首席设计师宋正宇表示，这也有望为大型和重型火箭的研发奠定基础，缩短研发周期并降低成本。
  新华社说，新火箭发射的五颗实验卫星将在和进行实验。 
北京在其太空计划上投入了大量资金，以表彰其技术实力和科学努力。  上周，一艘无人驾驶的中国航天器带着从月球上来的岩石和土壤回到了地球，这是40年来首次收集的月球样品。 引文：中国新的Long March-8火箭进行了首次飞行（2020年12月22日），2020年12月22日从https://phys.org/news/2020-12-china-march-rocket-flight.html检索到  本文件受版权保护。 除出于私下学习或研究目的进行的任何公平交易外，未经书面许可，不得复制任何内容。 内容仅供参考。
note_en:
China's new carrier rocket, the Long March-8, made its maiden flight on Tuesday, the country's space agency said, the first phase of a strategy to deploy launch vehicles that can be reused.    The Long March-8 series is part of China's endeavours to develop reusable rockets, potentially lowering mission costs and paving the way towards commercial launch services.
  The programme has drawn parallels to private US rocket firm SpaceX's Falcon range, although China said in 2018 its reusable carrier vehicle would use different technologies.
  The new medium-lift carrier rocket sent five satellites into planned orbit, blasting off from the Wenchang launch site on the southern Hainan island at 12:37 pm Beijing time (0437 GMT) on Tuesday.
  It measures 50.3 metres and has a take-off mass of 356 tonnes, and the China National Space Administration (CNSA) said it is of "great significance for accelerating the upgrading of launch vehicles".
  The rocket's design was based on technologies developed for previous Long March editions, Xinhua reported Tuesday.
  It is also expected to lay the foundation for development of large and heavy rockets, shortening development periods and reducing costs, said Song Zhengyu, the chief designer of the Long March-8.
  The five experimental satellites launched by the new rocket will conduct experiments in  ,   and  , said Xinhua.
  Beijing has invested heavily in its space programme as a sign of its technological prowess and scientific endeavour.
  An unmanned Chinese spacecraft returned to earth last week with rocks and soil from the moon—the first lunar samples collected in four decades.
